Optimistic mature nth-of-a-kind fusion scenario. Central values are calibrated to the ARPA-E-linked illustrative default plant reported by Handley et al. (capex about USD 2,366/kW, fixed O&M USD 8.52/kW-year, variable O&M USD 0.95/MWh, assumed zero fuel cost and 90% capacity factor). Values are rounded to EUR and represented as prospective Beta-PERT ranges; they are not observed commercial fusion costs.

Comparative grid-connected allocation: 40 MW at each terminal (80 MW total). This is lower than the 285 MW route-integrated Alpha Paper array so that the scenario retains a positive grid-import share under the daily simulation timestep.
Half of the 114 MWh route-level stationary storage allocation; 57 MWh is assigned to each California terminal.
Half of the 56 MW route-level stationary storage power allocation; 28 MW is assigned to each California terminal.

BKC Mumbai to Navi Mumbai terminal; distance reported in the corridor review.
Navi Mumbai to Wakad; assumed residual distance within the published 100--110 km commercial corridor.
Feasible daily schedule at the conservative 80 s headway bound: 24 h x 3600 / 80 = 1080 cycles per direction.
Route geometry and Western Ghats constrain operation to approximately 500 km/h.
Chosen to reproduce an approximately 25-minute end-to-end journey including station time.
Published proposal refers to 30 s; independent safety discussion suggests up to 80 s.
Assumption reflecting short segments, frequent acceleration, tropical cooling and station loads.
Model range based on Maharashtra industrial/EV tariffs, converted using an assumed 100 INR/EUR.
Range centred on the recent all-India grid emission intensity.
